title: Pharmaceutical technicians
description: Prepare medications under the direction of a pharmacist. May measure, mix, count out, label, and record amounts and dosages of medications according to prescription orders.

tasks.task: Receive written prescription or refill requests, verify that information is complete and accurate, and enter prescription information into computer databases.
Mix pharmaceutical preparations, according to written prescriptions, and transfer medication from vials to sterile, disposable syringes using aseptic techniques.
Restock intravenous (IV) supplies and add measured drugs or nutrients to IV solutions under sterile conditions to prepare IV packs.
Receive and store incoming supplies, verify quantities against invoices, check for outdated medications, and inform supervisors of stock needs and shortages.
Prepack bulk medicines, fill bottles with prescribed medications, price and file filled prescriptions, and type and affix labels.
Operate cash registers to accept payment, compute charges for medication or equipment dispensed, and prepare medical insurance claim forms.
Maintain proper storage and security conditions for drugs, and assist customers by answering simple questions or referring them to the pharmacist.
Clean and help maintain equipment or work areas and sterilize glassware according to prescribed methods.
Order, label, and count stock of medications or supplies and monitor robotic machines that dispense medicine into labeled containers.
Deliver medications or pharmaceutical supplies to patients, nursing stations, or surgeries, and answer telephones responding to questions or requests.

tools_used.name: Automatic unit dose strip packaging machines

detailed_work_activities.title: Maintain inventory of medical supplies or equipment.
Perform clerical work in medical settings.
Enter patient or treatment data into computers.
Enter information into databases or software programs.
Enter codes or other information into computers.
Provide information to the general public.
Provide customers with general information or assistance.
Arrange delivery of goods or services.
Monitor availability of equipment or supplies.
